# Start here: paper review

**Recommendation:** Not assessed

The Markdown reports and revision plan contain the full review; the local Review Desk is optional.

**Inventory:** 1 substantive comment, 1 editing comment, and 1 principal publication concern.

## Recommended reading order

1. Read the [referee report](report.md) for the overall assessment and publication risks.
2. Work through the [revision plan](fix-plan.md) from P0 to P2.
3. Use the [editing comments](editing-comments.md) for structure, terminology, mechanics, figures, and style.

## Highest-priority concerns

1. [The headline uniqueness result includes an unresolved boundary case](report.md#1-the-headline-uniqueness-result-includes-an-unresolved-boundary-case) — revision path: within current design.

## What was reviewed

- **Materials:** 1 source fully read; appendix not available.
- **Figures and equations:** figures not present; equations source preserved.

## What could not be checked

- No appendix was available for review.
- The review did not independently check the live literature.
- Replication code was not supplied, so the reported results were not rerun.
- No external literature claim was made.

## Review files

| Purpose | Open |
|---|---|
| Overview: Referee report | [Referee report](report.md) |
| Reports: Editing comments | [Editing comments](editing-comments.md) |
| Plan: Revision plan | [Revision plan](fix-plan.md) |

## Carry work into the next round

Use Review Desk to set your priority, add an instruction or response, and choose **Open**, **Ready for review**, or **Set aside** for each comment. Ready for review means either that a change was made or that a reasoned response is ready to assess. Set aside distinguishes comments to revisit next round from comments that do not apply or cannot be addressed. The exported revision brief carries these choices and your notes into the next round.
